name: Commercial State Bank, Of Wagner
description_short: Commercial State Bank is a community bank in Wagner, South Dakota, serving the local area since 1902 with personal and business banking services.
description_long:
Commercial State Bank was founded on May 31, 1902, and has operated continuously for over 120 years through multiple economic crises, including the 1907 panic, Great Depression, and 2008 financial crisis. The bank has maintained its original location at 204 S Main in Wagner, South Dakota, under the leadership of President Michael L. Frei since January 2014. The institution remains independent and focused on serving the Wagner community and surrounding areas with no plans for branch expansion.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking services. Personal offerings include checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, personal loans, debit and ATM cards, and multiple deposit calculators for financial planning. Business and agricultural customers can access business checking, business loans, and agricultural loans. The bank provides online banking and mobile app access for account management, and customers can make credit card payments through TCM Bank, N.A., the card services provider.

Commercial State Bank distinguishes itself through its long-standing community commitment and stability. The bank's mission explicitly focuses on promoting community development and improving conditions for underserved residents of economically distressed communities. Its century-plus operational history and survival through major economic downturns demonstrates institutional resilience. Management has remained within the founding Frei family in recent decades, suggesting continuity and local accountability.

As a single-location community bank, Commercial State Bank offers traditional banking services typical of regional institutions rather than specialized or innovative financial products. Customers should expect standard rates and offerings rather than competitive advantages in terms of technology or product breadth. The bank's strength lies in local relationship banking and community focus rather than digital-first services or comprehensive national reach. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Credit card services (through TCM Bank, N.A.)
Financial calculators (deposits, loans, retirement planning, debt payoff)
pros: Over 120 years of continuous operation and survived multiple major economic crises including the Great Depression and 2008 financial crisis
Independent community bank with explicit mission to support local community development and underserved populations
Online and mobile banking access available for account management convenience
Full range of personal and business services including checking, savings, CDs, loans, and agricultural lending
Established presence at 204 S Main in Wagner with stable, consistent management
Multiple deposit and loan calculators available to help customers with financial planning
ATM locations available throughout the service area with check ordering and online services
cons: Single location limits physical accessibility for customers outside the immediate Wagner area
No indication of competitive rates or special promotional offers compared to regional competitors
Limited digital innovation compared to larger banks or neobanks with modern app features
Credit card services outsourced to TCM Bank, N.A., suggesting limited in-house card program control
Website provides limited information about current rates, fees, or specific loan terms
best_for: Customers in Wagner, South Dakota and surrounding rural areas seeking relationship-based community banking
Agricultural and small business owners needing farm and business lending from a local lender
Individuals prioritizing banking stability and local community commitment over digital convenience
diagnosis: Commercial State Bank is best for customers in the Wagner, South Dakota area seeking traditional community banking with strong local roots and a commitment to serving underserved populations. The primary caveat is that this single-location institution lacks the digital innovation, broad geographic accessibility, and competitive rate shopping options available through larger regional or national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
